channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, Worldwide, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Artivism & Amplifiers Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Human Impacts Institute

Location: Global; pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South. Open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese.

Award: USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. Supported by expert workshops and creative mentors.

Duration: Six-month program.

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The Artivism & Amplifiers (A&A) Fellowship pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South to co-create multimedia campaigns, exhibits, and installations for the Creative Climate Awards 2027. Over six months, fellows build skills in intercultural collaboration, climate storytelling, and community engagement, supported by expert workshops and creative mentors. The program is open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. Fellows receive a USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. The organizers strongly encourage applications from people most impacted by the climate crisis, including those from the global majority, Indigenous, LGBTQIA+, immigrant, and diaspora communities, and people with disabilities. Applications close Wednesday 1 July 2026 at 9am GMT+2.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OIF: Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods 2026 (Francophonie)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF)

Location: Francophone zone (OIF member states globally)

Award: Up to EUR 5,000 for artist mobility (travel, accommodation, visas, logistics). Up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs). Both can be combined. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies or tours of 5 days to 3 months. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The OIF Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods programme provides grants of up to EUR 5,000 for mobility (travel, accommodation, visa fees, logistics) and up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs) within the Francophone zone; both can be combined. ELIGIBILITY: professional artists and established artistic collectives with a demonstrated connection to the Francophone space (by nationality, working language, or artistic collaborations), residing in an OIF member state, with minimum 2 years of experience in the cultural or artistic sector; must directly manage the project (not act as intermediaries); one application per individual per call. ELIGIBLE DISCIPLINES: live performance (music, theatre, dance, comedy, storytelling), cinema/audiovisual, literature/publishing, and visual arts. PRIORITY given to projects promoting gender equality, women artists, creators under 35, and South-South mobility. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026. Multiple selection sessions throughout the year; call open until 31 August 2026. Information webinars: 11 March, 13 May, and 8 July 2026 at 3 PM Paris time. Contact: culture.dlc@francophonie.org. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lee Ufan Arles: Prix Art & Environnement 2026 (Residency + Exhibition Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain

Location: Arles, France (residency and exhibition)

Award: Funded residency (production space + accommodation) in Arles. No cash prize amount specified. No application fee.

Duration: 6–8 weeks residency in early 2027, followed by a solo summer 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Prix Art & Environnement (4th edition), awarded by Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain, supports an artist working at the intersection of art and the natural environment. The prize winner receives a funded 6–8 week production residency in Arles in early 2027, including production space, accommodation, and mentorship from Lee Ufan Arles staff, culminating in a solo summer 2027 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists of any discipline and nationality, with no age or degree requirements; candidates must demonstrate at least one solo exhibition and one or more group exhibitions. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Google Form linked from the Lee Ufan Arles website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 (Documentary Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Women Photograph (supported by Leica USA)

Location: Remote (international)

Award: Three grants of USD 10,000 each (USD 30,000 total). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (new or in-progress photography projects).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 awards three grants of USD 10,000 each to support documentary photography projects. ELIGIBILITY: open to women, nonbinary, transgender, and intersex documentary photographers of any nationality; no membership required; no restrictions on camera equipment or career stage. Funding covers hard costs of reporting, photographers' creative fees, and any other expenses that support the production of new or in-progress still photography projects. Not intended for books, exhibitions, or workshops. Grantees retain full work ownership. One submission per applicant; applications in English (Google Translate acceptable); any use of generative AI must be disclosed. Applications opened 20 May 2026; deadline 30 June 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Apply at https://app.picter.com/contests/bad7ea97-7b0d-4a9c-9e85-38e73529d62b/start.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Async Museum: Open Call for Web-Based Art 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Async Museum

Location: Online (independent digital space); open to artists worldwide.

Award: Exhibition and visibility opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected web-based artworks are presented in Async Museum's independent online space. No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Rolling open call (exhibition in Async Museum's online space)

Deadline: 01 Dec 2026

Async Museum is an independent digital space dedicated to web art with a distinctive point of view, existing to discover artists who use the web as their primary medium and to recognise their work as a new aesthetic. Its core philosophy is that the web browser is a twenty-first century canvas. Async Museum is officially seeking submissions of web-based artworks, welcoming both established practitioners and emerging voices who are pushing the boundaries of what the next generation of art can be. NOTE: this is an exhibition opportunity for web-based art rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. To share your practice and submit your work, visit https://asyncmuseum.com/submit. Deadline: 1 December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prix Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize 2026 (OIF / ALECSO)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and Arab League Educational, Cultural and Scientific Organization (ALECSO)

Location: International (Francophone zone and Arab world)

Award: EUR 10,000 (funded equally by OIF and ALECSO). No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize (19th edition, 2026) is awarded jointly by the Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and ALECSO to recognise excellence in literary and humanities/social sciences translation between French and Arabic in either direction. Cash award: EUR 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual translators, universities and higher education institutions, research centres, national associations and unions, and publishing houses from the Arab world and Francophone space. Works must be literary or humanities/social sciences texts translated between French and Arabic (either direction), published within 3 years prior to the application year, and must be a first translation of the original. Self-published or photocopied works excluded. One work maximum per candidate. Applications submitted exclusively electronically; required materials: CV, presentation of translation work, copies of both translated and original texts. Deadline: 30 June 2026. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: eidolon Grant 2026 (Vernacular Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: eidolon

Location: International; open worldwide.

Award: A total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed across two application categories. No application fee.

Duration: Annual grant; supported projects contribute to the institution's program in the coming year

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The eidolon Grant is an international programme, presented annually, for projects that explore, promote and conserve vernacular photography. It is open to artists, academics, professionals, researchers, collectors and vernacular photography enthusiasts whose past work and proposed project is centred around the image heritage of everyday photography. The grant aims to identify phenomena, collections, histories, practices and trends within vernacular photography, offering new interpretations and analyses, and thematising both photographic heritages and contemporary photographic practices. Applications can be made under two categories, with a total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed. Each chosen project will contribute to the enrichment of eidolon's program in the coming year. The deadline for submission is 30 September 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Apply via https://everydayphotography.org/centre/grant-2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Cooper Prize 2026 (16th Street Foundation and Company 16, Melbourne)
item.description:
Organisation: 16th Street Foundation and Company 16

Location: Melbourne, Australia (international eligibility)

Award: AUD 20,000 prize to winner, plus consideration for a full commission and production of the winning work. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award); winning work may proceed to full commission and production.

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

The Cooper Prize 2026 is an international playwriting prize administered by 16th Street Foundation and Company 16 in Melbourne, Australia. AUD 20,000 is awarded to the winning playwright, plus consideration for a full commission and professional production. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. Open internationally to published and unpublished playwrights at any career stage with no geographic restrictions. Submissions must respond to thematic prompts drawn from Victor Frankl's 'Man's Search for Meaning' and Dostoevsky's 'The Brothers Karamazov', exploring themes including doubt versus faith, the meaning of suffering, and the power of truth. Application requires: (1) a one-page document covering motivation for writing, issues to explore, philosophical perspective or worldview, and a synopsis or treatment; (2) ten pages of the play manuscript. Judging panel led by Iain Sinclair and Harry Haynes. Deadline: 19 June 2026. Apply via JotForm at https://form.jotform.com/260408855140858.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AFAC: Music Grant 2026 (Arab Fund for Arts and Culture)
item.description:
Organisation: Arab Fund for Arts and Culture (AFAC)

Location: Arab region (open to individuals from the 22 Arab League states regardless of residence; institutions local, regional, and international working on Arab arts and culture)

Award: Annual grants of up to USD 25,000 for individuals and teams, and up to USD 35,000 for collectives and institutions. An artist or institutional fee of up to 30% of the total grant is an allowable expense. No application fee.

Duration: Projects to be completed within a maximum of two years from signature of the grant agreement

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

AFAC's Music grant supports music production, performances, collaborations, album recordings, music-related podcasts, and festivals. The program provides annual grants of up to USD 25,000 for individuals and teams, and up to USD 35,000 for collectives and institutions. WHO CAN APPLY: AFAC welcomes proposals from individuals from Arab countries (the 22 member states of the Arab League), regardless of place of residence, citizenship, or ethnic and national identification; it also accepts proposals from local, regional, and international institutions and organizations (artistic and cultural institutions, galleries, venues, and both nonprofit and for-profit entities) whose projects relate to arts and culture from the Arab region. INELIGIBLE: AFAC board, staff, their business partners or family, and current-year reader and juror committee members for the categories they evaluate; recipients of grants in two consecutive years are ineligible for the next two consecutive years; and applicants with a current open grant. AFAC launches two open calls a year; the second call covers Cinema, Music, and Training & Regional Events grants. Each applicant may submit only one application per open call. WHAT IT COVERS: project production including research and development, covering project-related expenses such as materials, space or equipment rentals, and fees for artistic and technical labor. An artist or institutional fee of up to 30% of the total grant is allowable. AFAC does not offer mobility grants, though project-related travel may be covered, and does not fund retroactively. PAYMENT: for grants above USD 10,000, applicants must show proof of at least 50% of the remaining project budget secured; payments are staged (50/35/15 for grants above USD 10,000, 70/30 for grants below). SELECTION: a grants management completeness review, then an independent readers committee, then an independent jury committee that reaches the final decision. AFAC does not own any material or moral rights to supported projects. Applications are accepted only through the online forms (separate forms for individuals, teams, collectives, and institutions). TIMELINE: deadline 19 June 2026 at 5:00 PM +3 GMT (Beirut time); announcement of selected projects 16 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Future Generation Art Prize 2027 (PinchukArtCentre)
item.description:
Organisation: Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre)

Location: Kyiv, Ukraine (exhibition of shortlisted artists at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027)

Award: Main prize US$100,000 ($60,000 cash + $40,000 investment in the winner's practice). Up to 5 Special Prizes share a total amount of US$20,000 for supporting projects that develop their artistic practice. No application fee.

Duration: Biannual prize cycle (shortlist Sept 2026; exhibition and award ceremony Spring 2027)

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

Biannual global contemporary art prize from the Victor Pinchuk Foundation (PinchukArtCentre, Kyiv), recognising and supporting a future generation of artists. The main winner receives US$100,000 ($60,000 cash plus $40,000 invested in their practice); up to five special prizes share a total of US$20,000 for projects developing the artist's practice. Shortlisted artists exhibit at PinchukArtCentre in Spring 2027, with the award ceremony following. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists aged 35 or younger worldwide, with no restrictions on gender, nationality, race or artistic medium; former Prize winners are not eligible to enter again but other previous applicants may re-apply. APPLY through the open call online. A selection committee reviews applications and selects up to twenty artists for the exhibition; in addition, 300 correspondent art experts worldwide nominate two to five candidates each. Applications accepted 11 May - 28 June 2026; shortlist announced 14 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Biafarin: BOAPS - Summer 2026 (international online art prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Biafarin

Location: Online (Canadian organisation; worldwide submissions and online exhibition)

Award: Cash grants per artist: Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists, total CAD 1,000 pool). NOTE: submission is free, but SELECTED ARTISTS PAY a USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early bird) to participate in the exhibition. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until exhibition opening day.

Duration: Online group exhibition 1 Jul - 30 Sep 2026 (3 months); up to 10 selected for separate 2-month online solo exhibitions

Deadline: Closed 09 Jun 2026

Seasonal international art prize from Biafarin (a Canadian artist-management platform), running a juried online group exhibition with cash grants, magazine/catalogue features, ARTSY presentation, and up to 10 solo online exhibitions. CASH GRANTS (modest): Major Grant CAD 500 (1 artist); Merit Grant CAD 250 each (4 artists); Recognition Grant CAD 100 each (10 artists). Plus up to 10 Honorable Mention certificates and various promotion benefits. ELIGIBILITY: open to all visual artists worldwide aged 18+, any 2D/3D medium (painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, photography, digital art, fiber, sculpture, mixed media); originals only, no copies/derivatives. SUBMISSION: free of charge. NOTE ON FEE: only SELECTED artists pay a fixed USD 50 selection fee (USD 40 early-bird) to participate in the exhibition; most selected artists pay this fee and do not receive a cash grant. Money-back guarantee on the selection fee until the official exhibition opening day. Up to 10 artworks per artist; max 2 may be selected. Deadline 9 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Belgrade Art Studio: Virtual Art Lab (12 Weeks, All Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Belgrade Art Studio

Location: Remote (online; English-language program; worldwide)

Award: PAID PROGRAM (artist pays): EUR 675 program fee. NOT a grant: no stipend, no bursary, no production budget. Includes 12 one-hour 1-on-1 mentor sessions, a final online viewing/discussion, and artist interview/promotion. No separate application fee.

Duration: 12 weeks / 3 months; weekly 1-hour individual sessions (Zoom or Google Meet); not full-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

PAID 12-week online mentorship program from Belgrade Art Studio: each artist works one-on-one with a dedicated artist-mentor through weekly 1-hour Zoom/Google Meet sessions, aimed at artists at a turning point in their practice (stuck, seeking new directions, experimenting with unfamiliar media including AI). Hybrid seminar/workshop format supporting both physical and online presentation, plus curatorial thinking, writing, interviews, and documentation. Closes with an online viewing and moderated discussion of the works produced. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to artists and creatives across visual arts, music, theatre, dance, film, writing, design, photography, digital/interactive art, games, fashion, architecture, and curation. English-language program (ability to collaborate in English preferred but accommodations possible). EXPECTATIONS: artists must treat the program as they would an in-person residency (dedicated time for research/creation/reflection), submit in-progress material for social-media exposure, participate in an artist interview, and refrain from hate speech or discriminatory content. APPLY by emailing CV, summary, and portfolio (links accepted) to artlab@belgradeartstudio.com. Rolling intake (no deadline). Selected artists are announced and contacted by email. COST: EUR 675 to participate — this is a paid offering, not a funded residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: .ART Award 2026 (Global Art Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: .ART (with Whitewall Magazine, Chateau du Fresne, Anfitrion)

Location: Hybrid mobility (Grand Prize cash + residencies in France and Spain)

Award: Grand Prize: USD 15,000 cash. Artist Residency Award (France): one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris. Artist Residency Award (Spain): residency at Anfitrion in Marbella. Publication Award: editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine (campaign media partner). .ART Domain Award: a USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name. No application fee mentioned (submission is via a .ART domain that documents the practice).

Duration: One-off open call; winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026; residency prizes scheduled separately

Deadline: 01 Nov 2026

Global art prize celebrating how art is made, open to artists worldwide working in any medium or art form. Instead of a traditional application form, artists submit a digital presence (website, portfolio or social profile) linked through a .ART domain that documents identity, process and work as one continuous record. PRIZES: Grand Prize (USD 15,000 cash); Artist Residency Award France (one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris); Artist Residency Award Spain (residency at Anfitrion, Marbella); Publication Award (editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine, the campaign's media partner); .ART Domain Award (USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name). ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and artist collectives from all backgrounds (cultures, ethnicities, gender identities, abilities); 18 or older at time of submission; open to artists from all geographic regions with no restrictions on nationality or place of residence. Multiple entries permitted, as long as each submission (single artwork, series or overall practice) is presented on or linked to a SEPARATE .ART domain. NOTE: a .ART domain is required for submission; this is the de facto entry mechanism. ANNOUNCEMENT: winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026. DEADLINE: 1 November 2026. APPLY via the .ART Award site.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Anolic Family Award

Location: Online application (open to early-career Jewish artists)

Award: $1,000 award for one early-career Jewish artist. No application fee.

Duration: One-off award; project supported within the award year, with a final artist statement and visual documentation due at year end

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Annual award providing momentum for early-career Jewish visual artists for a specific project. The 2026 cycle focuses on the fine arts: painting, drawing, sculpture and mixed media. Recipients must provide a brief artist statement and visual documentation of the supported work at year end, and must credit the Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award in any exhibition or publication of the supported work. ELIGIBILITY: active early-career Jewish artists aged 25 to 35; students may not apply. APPLY with a max 600-word proposal (why you're applying, why you're early-career, project/focus, how the award will help, how Judaism informs and is realised in your work - overt Jewish imagery is not required), 10 jpg images (max 1024px longest side, max 4 MB each), an image list, an up-to-2-page CV/resume, and an artist website (or social-media link if no site). Award announcement by September 2026. Questions: info@anolicfamilyaward.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
